steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 establishes that Gondwanan species originated ~80 million years ago when the island split. Text 2 gives crown ages for Grande Terre's current clades: stick insects at 41.1 Ma, nearly everything else more recent. The question asks what logically follows from combining these two texts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Most of them began recolonizing Grande Terre from nearby islands around 2.0 million years ago.: misapplies the Goodeniaceae date to Gondwanan origins — unsupported by either text. ✗
  • Choice B — Clades to which they belong originated no earlier than 41.1 million years ago.: reverses the logical relationship between clade age and species origin. ✗
  • Choice C — Few if any of them were members of a clade that includes species currently inhabiting Grande Terre.: a species from 80 Ma cannot belong to a clade whose crown age is only 41.1 Ma or younger — the clade didn't exist yet. ✓
  • Choice D — Although most of them have living descendants on Grande Terre, the stick insects and Goodeniaceae plants do not.: makes a specific unsupported claim not derivable from the crown-age data. ✗
That matches Choice C — Few if any of them were members of a clade that includes species currently inhabiting Grande Terre..
